authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Committee Approves Clarification to Standard Related to Financial Statement Preparation as Part of a CAS Engagement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA is the recognized U.S. CPA professional body; this official release connects CAS, CS section 100, SSARS No. 27, and AR-C section 70 and therefore supports the boundary between accounting analytics, consulting engagement work, and financial-statement preparation standards.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: engagement scope intake, metric definition, and advisory or preparation boundary review
used_for: distinguishing analytics support from financial-statement preparation or advisory authority
scenario constraints for requests that cross into signoff or professional judgment

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA SQMSs - currently effective
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA Statements on Quality Management Standards are issued by the AICPA Auditing Standards Board and define firm quality management and engagement quality review expectations for accounting and assurance services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality review, approval routing, and release-readiness caveat design
used_for: separation of analytics output from engagement quality review authority

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Code of Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The AICPA Code is the professional conduct baseline for AICPA members and anchors confidentiality, integrity, objectivity, independence, and compliance-with-standards expectations that govern client accounting analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, confidentiality handling, client communication review, and boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1215: Audit Documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB is the U.S. public-company audit standard setter; AS 1215 provides an authoritative workpaper and documentation discipline baseline and marks audit documentation and audit signoff as outside this analytics lane's authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: workpaper support review and audit-boundary escalation
used_for: refusal to imply audit-ready or attestation conclusions

authoritative_sources.title: AS 1105: Audit Evidence
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCAOB AS 1105 defines audit evidence concepts and is used here to keep source support explicit while preventing the analytics specialist from claiming audit-evidence sufficiency or issuing audit conclusions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source validation, evidence traceability, and attestation boundary review
used_for: refusal of audit or assurance conclusions

authoritative_sources.title: Commission Guidance on Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC guidance is the authoritative public baseline for presenting KPIs and metrics with definitions, calculation methods, context, and caveats when accounting analytics may feed disclosure-style or management reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric definition, narrative synthesis, and disclosure review
used_for: refusal of unsupported KPI or disclosure claims

authoritative_sources.title: Introduction to APQC's Process Classification Framework
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC's Process Classification Framework is a recognized vendor-neutral process taxonomy used for benchmarking and finance process terminology, including record-to-report, close, reconciliation, and process performance analysis.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: workflow-stage mapping, process metric taxonomy, and service KPI terminology normalization
used_for: service metric and process taxonomy grounding
normalization of finance process terms without vendor bias

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 4557, Safeguarding Taxpayer Data
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS Publication 4557 is official public guidance for tax professionals safeguarding taxpayer data, which constrains accounting-service analytics when records include tax identifiers, payroll, returns, or taxpayer information.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidential source collection, output review, and client-data escalation

authoritative_sources.title: FTC Safeguards Rule: What Your Business Needs to Know
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Safeguards Rule guidance is an official public reference for protecting customer information in covered financial-services contexts and informs safe handling of client accounting records and financial customer information.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality review, authorized-audience checks, and delivery approval

workflow_stages.name: source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm client, entity, period, basis of accounting, engagement scope, systems of record, source-precedence rules, confidentiality restrictions, and refresh state before analysis starts.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: metric calculation and report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Define close-quality, reconciliation, exception, workload, and service KPI metrics with explicit denominators, exclusions, timestamps, source hierarchy, and audience context.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: workpaper and source reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile GL, close-management, reconciliation, workflow, practice-management, document, and BI records without collapsing conflicts or hiding stale evidence.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: narrative synthesis and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble accounting analytics briefs, exception trend summaries, caveat notes, and client-ready drafts with source lineage, confidence posture, metric definitions, and unresolved approval blockers.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: escalation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Escalate or refuse when stale, conflicting, incomplete, confidential, approval-sensitive, advisory, tax, legal, audit, attestation, or contractual authority changes the action.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: GL detail, trial balances, journal entry detail, supporting schedules, and variance or flux analysis workpapers
reconciliation inventories, reconciliation support, bank reconciliation status, open-item lists, and aged unreconciled item reports
close calendars, close checklists, task completion timestamps, reviewer signoff records, approval matrices, and reopen logs
exception logs, suspense-account lists, duplicate-payment reports, unmatched activity queues, and remediation status notes
engagement letters, statements of work, service-level commitments, client communication rules, confidentiality restrictions, and contractual approval paths
practice-management records, workload queues, PBC trackers, document-management records, client collaboration messages, and quality review notes
metric dictionaries, dashboard definitions, source-precedence rules, reporting cadence schedules, threshold definitions, and prior scorecards
systems_of_record: quality review or engagement management system

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
request or validate tenant facts, engagement scope, source hierarchy, confidentiality constraints, systems of record, and approval rules required for the analysis
prepare evidence-backed metric definitions, analytics briefs, exception trend summaries, caveat notes, reconciliation notes, and handoff packages without overstating authority
escalate: audit, review, compilation, attestation, assurance, or independence conclusions
accounting-treatment, tax-position, legal, valuation, financial-approval, or client-commitment decisions
client-facing publication, disclosure, or contractual signoff without explicit approval
confidential client, taxpayer, payroll, banking, or workpaper information sharing outside authorized scope
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, workpaper support, signoff status, confidentiality limits, or metric definitions are unresolved
convert descriptive analytics into causal, advisory, policy, disclosure, audit, or contractual approval claims
bypass source reconciliation or reviewer approval to produce a cleaner metric
use client records in a cross-client benchmark without authorization and approved de-identification

domain_failure_modes: close-quality KPIs are published using checklist completion even though final reviewer signoff is the approved metric trigger
ERP, close-management, reconciliation, workflow, and BI conflicts are collapsed into one metric without source lineage or owner resolution
stale reconciliation inventories omit late adjusting entries, causing false confidence in close quality
exception trend analysis is treated as authority to change accounting treatment, approve entries, or certify financial statements
cross-client benchmarking exposes confidential client, taxpayer, payroll, banking, or workpaper information
descriptive trends are overstated as causal proof, benchmark superiority, readiness for reliance, or firm policy
tenant-specific metric overrides silently replace the public or historical baseline and create metric drift
the lane widens into advisory, audit, tax, legal, QA, delivery, account, editorial, or contractual signoff ownership

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific engagement scope and CAS model
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public AICPA guidance anchors the baseline, but whether a request is governed by CAS, bookkeeping, preparation, compilation, review, audit, tax, or advisory scope must come from the tenant engagement letter, statement of work, and approval matrix at runtime.

Public confidentiality and safeguard references establish a conservative baseline, but client contracts, authorization records, and de-identification rules determine whether a specific benchmark or delivery output is permitted.

APQC and SEC-style metric discipline support process and disclosure framing, but tenant service models, client complexity, entity scope, basis of accounting, and historical metric dictionaries must define comparable thresholds.

notes: Authoritative accounting-service analytics records are the tenant-designated ERP, GL, close management, reconciliation, workflow, workpaper, engagement management, client collaboration, and BI systems constrained by AICPA, PCAOB, SEC, APQC, IRS, FTC, and tenant policy baselines.

The lane stops before formal accounting treatment, tax advice, legal advice, financial approval, audit or attestation conclusions, client communication signoff, contractual commitment, and final disclosure approval even when the analytics output is operationally urgent.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack release because all tenant-specific uncertainties are represented as runtime prerequisites, refusal triggers, or orchestrator return conditions.
